title: Jerry Use Case Realization Schema
description: Validates YAML frontmatter for use case realization documents produced by /use-case, consumed by /test-spec and /contract-design. Shared artifact format per R-01 (phase-1-synthesis.md). Version 1.0.0: Identity/Classification/Actors/Flows blocks grounded in Cockburn (2001) and Jacobson (2011); Interactions block architecturally speculative pending Phase 3 validation gate.

id.description: Use case identifier. Format: UC-{DOMAIN}-{NNN} (e.g., UC-AUTH-001). Domain is an uppercase code (AUTH, KM, INV, etc.). (file-organization.md line 61)

title.description: Use case title following Cockburn naming: actor goal as title, verb-noun-context form (e.g., 'Validate User Credentials'). (Cockburn Ch. 3, S-02)

work_type.description: Immutable discriminator for AST H-33 compatibility. Always USE_CASE for use case realization documents. (file-organization.md line 63)

version.description: Semantic version of the artifact itself (not the schema). Increment MAJOR for breaking changes, MINOR for additions, PATCH for corrections. (file-organization.md line 64)

status.description: Artifact lifecycle status. DRAFT: initial creation. REVIEW: submitted for stakeholder review. APPROVED: accepted for implementation. DEPRECATED: superseded or abandoned. (file-organization.md line 65)

goal_level.description: Cockburn sea-metaphor goal classification. SUMMARY: Cloud/Kite level (organizational workflow). USER_GOAL: Sea Level (actor achieves measurable outcome). SUBFUNCTION: Fish/Clam level (supporting step within a user goal). 3 named levels collapse Cockburn's 5 levels for schema simplicity while preserving classification power. (Cockburn Ch. 5 p. 61, SD-02, DI-02)

goal_symbol.description: Cockburn goal-level annotation symbol. '+' = summary, '!' = user goal, '-' = subfunction. Compact display companion to goal_level enum. (Cockburn Reminders p. ii, SD-02)

detail_level.description: Jacobson UC 2.0 narrative detail level. Maps 1:1 to UC 2.0 progressive elaboration stages. BRIEFLY_DESCRIBED: name + goal + brief. BULLETED_OUTLINE: + MSS bullets (default). ESSENTIAL_OUTLINE: + extensions (minimum for /test-spec and /contract-design). FULLY_DESCRIBED: + handling + sub-UCs. (S-01, S-02, SD-03, DI-01, PAT-001)

scope.description: Design scope boundary per Cockburn (Enterprise, System, Subsystem). Identifies the system under discussion. (Cockburn Ch. 4, file-organization.md line 72)

domain.description: Short uppercase domain code (AUTH, KM, INV, etc.). Used in the id pattern and for grouping related use cases. (file-organization.md line 73)

primary_actor.description: The actor whose goal the use case satisfies. Typically a human role or external system. (Cockburn Ch. 2, file-organization.md line 76)

type.description: Actor classification. human: person in a role. system: internal system component. timer: time-triggered event. external: third-party service.

supporting_actors.description: Actors that participate in the use case but do not own the goal. cd-generator resolves supporting actor roles by cross-referencing interactions.actor_role with this array (IC-05 resolution). (file-organization.md lines 77-80, agent-decomposition-draft.md lines 142-146)

interest.description: What the stakeholder cares about in this use case. Per Cockburn Ch. 2: stakeholder interests define the guarantees the use case must satisfy.

stakeholders.description: Stakeholders with interests in the use case outcome. Optional for BRIEFLY_DESCRIBED level; SHOULD be populated at ESSENTIAL_OUTLINE+. (Cockburn Ch. 2)

preconditions.description: Conditions that must be true before the use case begins. The system does not check these -- they are assumed true. (Cockburn Ch. 6)

success.description: Conditions guaranteed to be true when the basic flow completes successfully. AKA success guarantees.

failure.description: Minimum conditions guaranteed to be true even when the use case fails. AKA minimal guarantees.

postconditions.description: Postconditions for success and failure paths. Cockburn distinguishes success guarantees (met when MSS completes) from minimum guarantees (met even on failure). (Cockburn Ch. 6)

trigger.description: The event that initiates the use case. (Cockburn Ch. 6)

slice_state.description: UC 2.0 five-state slice lifecycle. SCOPED: slice identified from flows. PREPARED: test cases defined, narrative enhanced. ANALYZED: system elements identified, realization produced (Activity 5). IMPLEMENTED: software built and unit tested. VERIFIED: acceptance tests passed. Uses UC 2.0 baseline (5 states); UC 3.0 sixth state deferrable via minor schema version bump. (S-01 pp. 15-16, SD-04, DI-03, PAT-006)

basic_flow.description: Main Success Scenario steps. Cockburn Guideline 6: 3-9 steps indicate correct abstraction level. Each step is an actor action or system response. The basic flow is the happy path. (Cockburn Ch. 7 p. 93, S-02, file-organization.md lines 89-98)

alternative_flows.description: Flows that branch from and optionally rejoin the basic flow. Each alternative represents a different way to achieve the use case goal. (Cockburn Ch. 8, file-organization.md lines 100-111)

extensions.description: Step-anchored exception and error handling flows. Each extension is triggered by a condition at a specific basic_flow step. Extensions that terminate in failure become negative test scenarios in /test-spec (PAT-008, Clark 2018). (Cockburn Ch. 8, file-organization.md lines 113-124)

$comment_interactions.const: === INTERACTIONS BLOCK (/contract-design primary consumer; produced by uc-slicer Activity 5) — ARCHITECTURALLY SPECULATIVE: Phase 3 validation gate required before production adoption. See file-organization.md 'Interactions Block Validation Gate' section. ===

interactions.description: Derived from flows by uc-slicer (Activities 4-5). Each interaction represents a system boundary crossing where a system_response step implies an API boundary. This block is the primary input for cd-generator. ARCHITECTURALLY SPECULATIVE: This block is designed for /contract-design's novel transformation algorithm (G-01, no prior art). The schema defines the structure but the block requires validation via the Phase 3 validation gate (file-organization.md 'Interactions Block Validation Gate' section) before production adoption. Validation criterion: 3 representative use cases (2+ domains, 1+ with supporting actors) must produce valid OpenAPI from this block alone without cd-generator inferring unstated structure. supporting_actor role mapping: cd-generator resolves supporting actor roles by cross-referencing interaction actor_role with the top-level supporting_actors array (IC-05 resolution). (S-01 Activity 5, file-organization.md lines 126-146, agent-decomposition-draft.md lines 288-365)

slices.description: Use case slices per Jacobson UC 2.0 Activity 2. Each slice is an implementation-ready subset of the use case containing end-to-end behavior through the concept. (S-01, DI-03, agent-decomposition-draft.md uc-slicer methodology)

parent_id.description: Parent use case ID when this is a subfunction goal. Null for top-level use cases. (file-organization.md line 149)

related_use_cases.description: References to related use cases by ID. (file-organization.md line 150)

requirements.description: References to external requirements (e.g., REQ-001). Enables bidirectional traceability between use cases and requirements. (file-organization.md line 151)

slice_ids.description: References to slice documents by ID. Format: UC-{DOMAIN}-{NNN}-S{N}. (file-organization.md line 152)

priority.description: Implementation priority. P0: critical path. P1: high priority. P2: medium. P3: low/deferred. (file-organization.md line 155)

created_at.description: ISO-8601 datetime of artifact creation. (file-organization.md line 156)

updated_at.description: ISO-8601 datetime of last modification. (file-organization.md line 157)

created_by.description: Agent or human author who created the artifact. (file-organization.md line 158)

last_modified_by.description: Agent or human who last modified the artifact.

realization_level.description: Top-level convenience field indicating the overall realization depth. OUTLINED: flows exist but no interactions. STORY_DEFINED: slices defined with test cases. INTERACTION_DEFINED: Activity 5 realization complete with interactions block. This field is a derived summary; the authoritative state is determined by the presence and completeness of the flows, slices, and interactions blocks.

step.description: Step number within the flow. Sequential, starting from 1.

actor.description: The actor performing this step. Must match primary_actor, a supporting_actors[].name entry, or 'System'.

action.description: What the actor does. Verb-object form (e.g., 'submits credentials', 'validates token').

type.description: Step type classification for Clark transformation. actor_action: human/external actor performs an action (maps to Gherkin When). system_response: system processes and responds (maps to Gherkin Then). validation: system validates input/state (maps to Gherkin Then with assertion). (PAT-008, SD-07, DI-05)

name.description: Descriptive name of the alternative flow.

branches_from_step.description: Step number in basic_flow where this alternative branches.

condition.description: The condition that triggers this alternative path. Per Cockburn Guideline 11: 'what was detected'. (Cockburn Ch. 8)

rejoins_at_step.description: Step number in basic_flow where this alternative rejoins. Null if this flow reaches a separate exit (success or failure).

id.description: Extension identifier using Cockburn step-anchor numbering. Format: EXT-{step}{letter} (e.g., EXT-3a). (Cockburn Ch. 8)

anchor_step.description: The basic_flow step number this extension is anchored to.

condition.description: The exception or error condition detected at the anchor step.

outcome.description: How the extension terminates. 'success': reaches a separate successful exit. 'failure': use case fails (maps to negative test scenario in /test-spec). 'rejoin:{step}': returns to basic_flow at the specified step. Clark mapping: outcome=failure becomes negative test scenario; outcome=rejoin becomes additional scenario. (PAT-008, SD-08, DI-05)

source_step.description: References a step number in the source flow. Enables traceability from API contract operation back to use case step. (DI-08, R-06)

source_flow.description: Which flow the source_step belongs to. basic_flow for main success scenario, AF-{NN} for alternatives, EXT-{anchor}{letter} for extensions. (file-organization.md line 132)

actor_role.description: The role of the actor in this interaction. consumer: the actor initiates the request (maps to API caller). provider: the actor provides a service (maps to API server). cd-generator uses this to determine operation directionality. (R-06, agent-decomposition-draft.md actor-to-contract-role mapping)

system_role.description: The role of the system in this interaction. receiver: system receives and processes requests. provider: system provides data/services. (file-organization.md line 134)

request_description.description: What the actor sends to the system. Becomes the basis for request body/parameter schemas in OpenAPI generation. (file-organization.md line 135, agent-decomposition-draft.md step 5)

response_description.description: What the system returns. Becomes the basis for response schemas in OpenAPI generation. (file-organization.md line 136, agent-decomposition-draft.md step 6)

preconditions.description: Conditions that must be true before this interaction executes. Map to request validation constraints in OpenAPI generation. (file-organization.md lines 137-138, agent-decomposition-draft.md step 5)

postconditions.description: Conditions guaranteed after this interaction completes. Map to response schema guarantees in OpenAPI generation. (file-organization.md lines 139-140, agent-decomposition-draft.md step 6)

interaction.description: A single interaction boundary identified from use case flows by uc-slicer during Activity 5. Each system_response step that implies an API boundary produces one interaction. ARCHITECTURALLY SPECULATIVE: see parent interactions array description for validation gate details.

slice_id.description: Slice identifier. Format: UC-{DOMAIN}-{NNN}-S{N} (e.g., UC-AUTH-001-S1). (file-organization.md line 152)

flow.description: Which flow the steps belong to.

steps.description: Step numbers included from the referenced flow.

steps_included.description: The flow steps included in this slice. Each entry references a flow and the step numbers from that flow.

realization_level.description: The depth of realization for this specific slice. OUTLINED: slice identified, flows selected. STORY_DEFINED: test cases defined, narrative at ESSENTIAL_OUTLINE. INTERACTION_DEFINED: Activity 5 complete, interactions block produced for this slice's steps.

test_cases.description: Acceptance test case descriptions for this slice. Required before transitioning from SCOPED to PREPARED state. (S-01 Activity 4, agent-decomposition-draft.md uc-slicer methodology step 6)

invest_assessment.description: INVEST criteria assessment per Jacobson UC 2.0 Activity 2. Each boolean indicates whether the criterion is satisfied. (agent-decomposition-draft.md uc-slicer methodology step 3)

allOf.$comment: Cross-field constraint: interactions block is only expected at INTERACTION_DEFINED realization level
Cross-field constraint: slices expected at STORY_DEFINED or INTERACTION_DEFINED realization level
Cross-field constraint: goal_symbol must be consistent with goal_level
Cross-field constraint: goal_symbol must be consistent with goal_level
Cross-field constraint: goal_symbol must be consistent with goal_level
